---
title: "About This Book"
---

# About This Book {.unnumbered}

**Thinking with Cases: An Introduction to Bioethics**

Brendan Shea, PhD

*Real medical dilemmas, taken apart — so you can decide what you think.*

---

This book is an open introduction to bioethics built around true stories of medicine at its hardest — moments where thoughtful people, acting in good faith, reached the edge of what their training could settle and had to decide anyway. The opening chapter equips a reader coming to the field fresh with the essential toolkit: the four principles of biomedical ethics, the moral theories that sit behind them, the short and brutal history that produced our rules of consent and research, and the simple skill of taking an argument apart to find the premise in dispute. The chapters that follow put those tools to work, one case at a time. Each gives every side its strongest voice and ends not with a verdict but with an open question — because the aim is not to tell you what to think, but to make you a better thinker.

Begin with **the introduction**, which explains the ideas and how to use the cases. After that, the cases can be read in any order.

## Copyright and License

Copyright © Brendan Shea.

Published by the **Minnesota Libraries Publishing Project**. The online edition is available at <https://mlpp.pressbooks.pub/bioethics/>.

This work is an **open educational resource**, released under a [Creative Commons Attribution–NonCommercial 4.0 International (CC BY-NC 4.0) License](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/4.0/). You are free to **share** (copy and redistribute in any medium or format) and to **adapt** (remix, transform, and build upon the material) for any non-commercial purpose, provided you give appropriate credit, link to the license, and indicate if changes were made. You may not use the material for commercial purposes.

This book is a work of human–artificial intelligence collaboration; the introduction explains how it was made and who is accountable for it.

The collection is meant to grow as medicine raises new dilemmas. This edition was generated from the source materials on the date shown.
